reasoning: The control AC-6(8) focuses on enforcing least privilege principles, which directly maps to Kubernetes RBAC configurations to prevent excessive permissions. Specifically, ensuring that no cluster-wide admin roles are bound unnecessarily, enforcing least privilege by carefully defining roles and bindings, and disabling service account token automounting by default are key practices. Additionally, ensuring that pods do not run as the root user aligns with the principle of least privilege by restricting unnecessary privileges at the pod level.
